content.text: A session-scoped Stop hook is now active with condition: "simplify this repo, make sure to de-slop it continue until you are happy, make sure to backfill tests and validate any significant change. The output of this should be a PR with green CI, reviewed that simplify and makes the codebase more robust and elegant, without dropping any features. Work on one branch for now but make commits that could later be split into stacked PRs for better reviewing.". Briefly acknowledge the goal, then immediately start (or continue) working toward it — treat the condition itself as your directive and do not pause to ask the user what to do. The hook will block stopping until the condition holds. It auto-clears once the condition is met — do not tell the user to run `/goal clear` after success; that's only for clearing a goal early.
Goal acknowledged: simplify and de-slop this repo, backfill tests, validate changes, and end with a reviewed PR with green CI. Let me start by surveying the codebase.

input.description: Map internal packages for slop
input.prompt:
You are analyzing the Go repo at /Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/git-sync (a git repo sync library + CLI). Your job: thoroughly read the packages under internal/ — especially internal/syncer, internal/planner, internal/strategy (bootstrap, incremental, materialized), internal/gitproto, internal/internalbridge, internal/validation, internal/auth, internal/useragent — and report concrete simplification/de-slop opportunities WITHOUT dropping features.

Look specifically for:
1. Dead code: unexported functions/types/fields never referenced, exported identifiers in internal packages never used outside their package (verify with grep across the whole repo).
2. Duplicated or near-duplicated logic (same helper implemented in multiple packages, copy-pasted blocks).
3. Over-abstraction: interfaces with a single implementation, wrapper types that only mirror another type field-for-field (e.g. internalbridge mirroring public types), indirection layers that add no value, config structs with unused fields.
4. Overly defensive code: impossible error checks, redundant validation done at multiple layers.
5. Verbose slop patterns: functions with 8+ params that should take a struct, giant switch statements duplicating data tables, comments that just restate code.
6. Unused struct fields (grep for reads, not just writes).

For each finding give: file:line, what it is, why it's slop, and a concrete safe simplification. Rank findings by impact (lines removable / clarity gained) and confidence that removal is behavior-preserving. Verify each claim with grep before reporting — do not report speculative findings. Return a structured report; it will be used to plan refactoring commits. Aim for the 10-25 highest-value findings, verified.

input.description: Map cmd/ CLI for slop
input.prompt:
You are analyzing the Go repo at /Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/git-sync (a git repo sync library + CLI). Your job: thoroughly read cmd/git-sync (all files: main.go, root.go, flags.go, fetch.go, probe.go, syncplan.go, bootstrap.go, convert_sha256.go, version.go, internal/versioninfo, internal/sha256convert) and cmd/git-sync-bench/main.go, and report concrete simplification/de-slop opportunities WITHOUT dropping features or changing CLI behavior/flags.

Look specifically for:
1. Dead code: functions/types never referenced (verify with grep across the whole repo), flags parsed but never used, env vars read but ignored.
2. Duplicated logic between subcommands (repeated config assembly, repeated auth resolution, copy-pasted validation) that could be factored into one helper.
3. Over-abstraction: needless indirection, single-implementation interfaces, structs mirroring other structs field-for-field.
4. sha256convert is 1862 lines — look hard for repetition, dead branches, and helpers that could collapse. Same for git-sync-bench (500 lines).
5. Overly defensive code and redundant validation layers.
6. Comments that restate code, stale comments, TODO/FIXME that are already done.

For each finding give: file:line, what it is, why it's slop, and a concrete safe simplification. Rank by impact and confidence that the change is behavior-preserving. Verify each claim with grep before reporting — no speculative findings. Return a structured report of the 10-20 highest-value findings.

input.description: Map public API for slop
input.prompt:
You are analyzing the Go repo at /Users/soph/Work/entire/devenv/git-sync (a git repo sync library + CLI, module entire.io/entire/git-sync). Your job: analyze the public API surface — root package files (client.go, types.go, errors.go, doc.go), unstable/ package, and internal/internalbridge — and report concrete simplification/de-slop opportunities.

Key questions:
1. internal/internalbridge appears to exist so the root package can call internal/syncer while mirroring every type field-for-field (Endpoint, EndpointAuth, RefScope, SyncPolicy, RefMapping, OperationMode, ProtocolMode...). Map exactly what internalbridge contains, what it duplicates, and whether the root package could call internal/syncer (or a slimmer layer) directly — check whether internalbridge does ANY real work (transformations, defaults, validation) or is pure pass-through. List every function and whether it's pure mirroring.
2. unstable/client.go (400 lines): what does it expose, who uses it (grep repo-wide + check docs), how much does it duplicate the stable client? Note: it's a published Go module so external users may exist — flag what could be shared internally without breaking its API.
3. Duplicate validation between root package Validate() methods and internal/validation.
4. errors.go / types.go: exported symbols never produced or consumed anywhere (verify with grep).
5. example_test.go, doc.go: stale docs vs actual API.
6. Check docs/*.md accuracy w.r.t. code as a secondary item.

For each finding give: file:line, what it is, why it's slop, and a concrete safe simplification that preserves the PUBLIC API (root package + unstable are published; internal/ can change freely). Verify claims with grep. Return a structured report ranked by impact.

59		Bytes       int64  `json:"bytes"`
60		Display     string `json:"display,omitempty"`
61		ActiveNanos int64  `json:"activeNanos,omitempty"`
62		IdleNanos   int64  `json:"idleNanos,omitempty"`
63	}
64	
65	type Stats struct {
66		Enabled      bool                     `json:"enabled"`
67		Items        map[string]*ServiceStats `json:"items"`
68		Sides        []SideBytes              `json:"sides,omitempty"`
69		ElapsedNanos int64                    `json:"elapsedNanos,omitempty"`
70	}
71	
72	type Measurement struct {
73		Enabled            bool   `json:"enabled"`
74		ElapsedMillis      int64  `json:"elapsedMillis"`
75		PeakAllocBytes     uint64 `json:"peakAllocBytes"`
76		PeakHeapInuseBytes uint64 `json:"peakHeapInuseBytes"`
77		TotalAllocBytes    uint64 `json:"totalAllocBytes"`
78		GCCount            uint32 `json:"gcCount"`
79	}
80	
81	type ProbeResult struct {
82		SourceURL     string      `json:"sourceUrl"`
83		TargetURL     string      `json:"targetUrl,omitempty"`
84		RequestedMode string      `json:"requestedMode"`
85		Protocol      string      `json:"protocol"`
86		RefPrefixes   []string    `json:"refPrefixes"`
87		Capabilities  []string    `json:"sourceCapabilities"`
88		TargetCaps    []string    `json:"targetCapabilities,omitempty"`
89		Refs          []RefInfo   `json:"refs"`
90		SourceHEAD    string      `json:"sourceHead,omitempty"`
91		Stats         Stats       `json:"stats"`
92		Measurement   Measurement `json:"measurement"`
93	}
94	
95	type SyncCounts struct {
96		Applied int `json:"applied"`
97		Skipped int `json:"skipped"`
98		Blocked int `json:"blocked"`
99		Deleted int `json:"deleted"`
100		Warned  int `json:"warned"`
101	}
102	
103	type BatchSummary struct {
104		Enabled bool `json:"enabled"`
105		Planned int  `json:"planned"`
106		Done    int  `json:"done"`
107	}
108	
109	type ExecutionSummary struct {
110		DryRun             bool         `json:"dryRun"`
111		Protocol           string       `json:"protocol"`
112		OperationMode      string       `json:"operationMode"`
113		Relay              bool         `json:"relay"`
114		TransferMode       string       `json:"transferMode"`
115		Reason             string       `json:"reason"`
116		BootstrapSuggested bool         `json:"bootstrapSuggested"`
117		SourceHEAD         string       `json:"sourceHead,omitempty"`
118		Batch              BatchSummary `json:"batch"`
119	}
120	
121	type SyncResult struct {
122		Refs        []RefResult      `json:"refs"`
123		Counts      SyncCounts       `json:"counts"`
124		Execution   ExecutionSummary `json:"execution"`
125		Stats       Stats            `json:"stats"`
126		Measurement Measurement      `json:"measurement"`
127	}
128	
129	type PlanResult = SyncResult
130	
131	func FromProbeResult(result syncer.ProbeResult) ProbeResult {
132		out := ProbeResult{
133			SourceURL:     result.SourceURL,
134			TargetURL:     result.TargetURL,
135			RequestedMode: result.RequestedMode,
136			Protocol:      result.Protocol,
137			RefPrefixes:   append([]string(nil), result.RefPrefixes...),
138			Capabilities:  append([]string(nil), result.Capabilities...),
139			TargetCaps:    append([]string(nil), result.TargetCaps...),
140			Refs:          make([]RefInfo, 0, len(result.Refs)),
141			SourceHEAD:    result.SourceHEAD.String(),
142			Stats:         FromStats(result.Stats),
143			Measurement:   FromMeasurement(result.Measurement),
144		}
145		for _, ref := range result.Refs {
146			out.Refs = append(out.Refs, RefInfo{Name: ref.Name, Hash: ref.Hash.String()})
147		}
148		return out
149	}
150	
151	func FromSyncResult(result syncer.Result) SyncResult {
152		out := SyncResult{
153			Refs: make([]RefResult, 0, len(result.Plans)),
154			Counts: SyncCounts{
155				Applied: result.Pushed,
156				Skipped: result.Skipped,
157				Blocked: result.Blocked,
158				Deleted: result.Deleted,
159				Warned:  result.Warned,
160			},
161			Execution: ExecutionSummary{
162				DryRun:             result.DryRun,
163				Protocol:           result.Protocol,
164				OperationMode:      result.OperationMode,
165				Relay:              result.Relay,
166				TransferMode:       result.RelayMode,
167				Reason:             result.RelayReason,
168				BootstrapSuggested: result.BootstrapSuggested,
169				SourceHEAD:         result.SourceHEAD.String(),
170				Batch: BatchSummary{
171					Enabled: result.Batching,
172					Planned: result.PlannedBatchCount,
173					Done:    result.BatchCount,
174				},
175			},
176			Stats:       FromStats(result.Stats),
177			Measurement: FromMeasurement(result.Measurement),
178		}
179		for _, plan := range result.Plans {
180			out.Refs = append(out.Refs, RefResult{
181				Branch:     plan.Branch,
182				SourceRef:  plan.SourceRef.String(),
183				TargetRef:  plan.TargetRef.String(),
184				SourceHash: HashString(plan.SourceHash),
185				TargetHash: HashString(plan.TargetHash),
186				Kind:       RefKind(plan.Kind),
187				Action:     Action(plan.Action),
188				Reason:     plan.Reason,
189			})
190		}
191		return out
192	}
193	
194	func FromStats(stats syncer.Stats) Stats {
195		out := Stats{Enabled: stats.Enabled, Items: make(map[string]*ServiceStats, len(stats.Items))}
196		for key, item := range stats.Items {
197			copyItem := *item
198			out.Items[key] = &ServiceStats{
199				Name:          copyItem.Name,
200				Requests:      copyItem.Requests,
201				RequestBytes:  copyItem.RequestBytes,
202				ResponseBytes: copyItem.ResponseBytes,
203				Wants:         copyItem.Wants,
204				Haves:         copyItem.Haves,
205				Commands:      copyItem.Commands,
206			}
207		}
208		if len(stats.Sides) > 0 {
209			out.Sides = make([]SideBytes, 0, len(stats.Sides))
210			for _, side := range stats.Sides {
211				out.Sides = append(out.Sides, SideBytes{
212					Label:       side.Label,
213					Bytes:       side.Bytes,
214					Display:     side.Display,
215					ActiveNanos: side.ActiveNanos,
216					IdleNanos:   side.IdleNanos,
217				})
218			}
219		}
220		out.ElapsedNanos = stats.ElapsedNanos
221		return out
222	}
223	
224	func FromMeasurement(m syncer.Measurement) Measurement {
225		return Measurement{
226			Enabled:            m.Enabled,
227			ElapsedMillis:      m.ElapsedMillis,
228			PeakAllocBytes:     m.PeakAllocBytes,
229			PeakHeapInuseBytes: m.PeakHeapInuseBytes,
230			TotalAllocBytes:    m.TotalAllocBytes,
231			GCCount:            m.GCCount,
232		}
233	}
234	
235	func HashString(hash plumbing.Hash) string {
236		if hash.IsZero() {
237			return ""
238		}
239		return hash.String()
240	}
241

result.output:
1	package gitsync
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"errors"
6	
7		"entire.io/entire/git-sync/internal/internalbridge"
8	)
9	
10	// ProtocolMode controls source-side protocol negotiation.
11	type ProtocolMode string
12	
13	const (
14		ProtocolAuto ProtocolMode = "auto"
15		ProtocolV1   ProtocolMode = "v1"
16		ProtocolV2   ProtocolMode = "v2"
17	)
18	
19	// OperationMode controls high-level sync semantics.
20	type OperationMode string
21	
22	const (
23		ModeSync      OperationMode = "sync"
24		ModeReplicate OperationMode = "replicate"
25	)
26	
27	// Endpoint identifies a remote Git endpoint.
28	type Endpoint struct {
29		URL string `json:"url"`
30	
31		// FollowInfoRefsRedirect, when true, rewrites this endpoint's
32		// effective host to the final URL returned by /info/refs after
33		// HTTP redirects. Subsequent git RPCs (git-upload-pack,
34		// git-receive-pack) then target the redirected host directly.
35		// Matches vanilla git's smart-HTTP behaviour for discovery-aware
36		// servers that 307 /info/refs to a hosting replica.
37		FollowInfoRefsRedirect bool `json:"followInfoRefsRedirect,omitempty"`
38	}
39	
40	// EndpointAuth carries explicit per-request auth and TLS settings.
41	// It is resolved through an AuthProvider rather than embedded in Endpoint so
42	// endpoint identity does not also become the public auth-precedence boundary.
43	type EndpointAuth struct {
44		Username      string `json:"username"`
45		Token         string `json:"token"`
46		BearerToken   string `json:"bearerToken"`
47		SkipTLSVerify bool   `json:"skipTlsVerify"`
48	}
49	
50	// EndpointRole identifies whether auth is being resolved for the source or target.
51	type EndpointRole string
52	
53	const (
54		SourceRole EndpointRole = "source"
55		TargetRole EndpointRole = "target"
56	)
57	
58	// AuthProvider resolves auth for a request endpoint.
59	type AuthProvider interface {
60		AuthFor(ctx context.Context, endpoint Endpoint, role EndpointRole) (EndpointAuth, error)
61	}
62	
63	// StaticAuthProvider returns fixed source and target auth values.
64	type StaticAuthProvider struct {
65		Source EndpointAuth `json:"source"`
66		Target EndpointAuth `json:"target"`
67	}
68	
69	// AuthFor implements AuthProvider.
70	func (p StaticAuthProvider) AuthFor(_ context.Context, _ Endpoint, role EndpointRole) (EndpointAuth, error) { //nolint:unparam // implements AuthProvider interface
71		if role == TargetRole {
72			return p.Target, nil
73		}
74		return p.Source, nil
75	}
76	
77	// RefMapping is an explicit source-to-target ref mapping.
78	type RefMapping struct {
79		Source string `json:"source"`
80		Target string `json:"target"`
81	}
82	
83	// RefScope constrains which refs a request manages. AllRefs broadens scope
84	// to every refs/* on the source (branches, tags, notes, pulls, custom
85	// namespaces) and implies SyncPolicy.IncludeTags. ExcludeRefPrefixes
86	// subtracts namespaces from auto-discovery (useful for trimming
87	// refs/pull/* when mirroring open-source GitHub repos under AllRefs);
88	// explicit Mappings are not subject to it.
89	type RefScope struct {
90		Branches           []string     `json:"branches"`
91		Mappings           []RefMapping `json:"mappings"`
92		AllRefs            bool         `json:"allRefs,omitempty"`
93		ExcludeRefPrefixes []string     `json:"excludeRefPrefixes,omitempty"`
94	}
95	
96	// SyncPolicy controls high-level sync behavior. BestEffort downgrades per-ref
97	// receive-pack rejections to warnings; pack-level failures remain fatal.
98	//
99	// ForceWithLease and ForceBlind both allow non-fast-forward branch updates and
100	// tag retargets; they differ in how the push command's expected-old value is
101	// set. ForceWithLease sends the target tip captured at session start, so
102	// receive-pack rejects updates where the target moved during the run (the
103	// "lease"). ForceBlind sends a zero expected-old, telling receive-pack to
104	// overwrite regardless of the current target value — matching `git push
105	// --force` semantics. The two are mutually exclusive.
106	type SyncPolicy struct {
107		Mode           OperationMode `json:"mode"`
108		IncludeTags    bool          `json:"includeTags"`
109		ForceWithLease bool          `json:"forceWithLease,omitempty"`
110		ForceBlind     bool          `json:"forceBlind,omitempty"`
111		Prune          bool          `json:"prune"`
112		BestEffort     bool          `json:"bestEffort,omitempty"`
113		Protocol       ProtocolMode  `json:"protocol"`
114	}
115	
116	// Validate enforces SyncPolicy invariants at the request edge.
117	func (p SyncPolicy) Validate() error {
118		if p.ForceWithLease && p.ForceBlind {
119			return errors.New("ForceWithLease and ForceBlind are mutually exclusive")
120		}
121		if p.Mode == ModeReplicate && (p.ForceWithLease || p.ForceBlind) {
122			return errors.New("replicate does not support force flags; use sync instead")
123		}
124		return nil
125	}
126	
127	// ProbeRequest inspects source refs and optional target capabilities.
128	type ProbeRequest struct {
129		Source             Endpoint     `json:"source"`
130		Target             *Endpoint    `json:"target"`
131		IncludeTags        bool         `json:"includeTags"`
132		AllRefs            bool         `json:"allRefs,omitempty"`
133		ExcludeRefPrefixes []string     `json:"excludeRefPrefixes,omitempty"`
134		Protocol           ProtocolMode `json:"protocol"`
135		CollectStats       bool         `json:"collectStats"`
136	}
137	
138	// PlanRequest computes ref actions without pushing.
139	type PlanRequest struct {
140		Source       Endpoint   `json:"source"`
141		Target       Endpoint   `json:"target"`
142		Scope        RefScope   `json:"scope"`
143		Policy       SyncPolicy `json:"policy"`
144		CollectStats bool       `json:"collectStats"`
145	}
146	
147	// SyncRequest executes a sync between two remotes.
148	type SyncRequest struct {
149		Source       Endpoint   `json:"source"`
150		Target       Endpoint   `json:"target"`
151		Scope        RefScope   `json:"scope"`
152		Policy       SyncPolicy `json:"policy"`
153		CollectStats bool       `json:"collectStats"`
154	}
155	
156	type RefKind = internalbridge.RefKind
157	
158	const (
159		RefKindBranch RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindBranch
160		RefKindTag    RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindTag
161		RefKindOther  RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindOther
162	)
163	
164	type Action = internalbridge.Action
165	
166	const (
167		ActionCreate Action = internalbridge.ActionCreate
168		ActionUpdate Action = internalbridge.ActionUpdate
169		ActionDelete Action = internalbridge.ActionDelete
170		ActionSkip   Action = internalbridge.ActionSkip
171		ActionBlock  Action = internalbridge.ActionBlock
172		ActionWarn   Action = internalbridge.ActionWarn
173	)
174	
175	type RefResult = internalbridge.RefResult
176	type RefPlan = internalbridge.RefPlan
177	type RefInfo = internalbridge.RefInfo
178	type ServiceStats = internalbridge.ServiceStats
179	type Stats = internalbridge.Stats
180	type Measurement = internalbridge.Measurement
181	type ProbeResult = internalbridge.ProbeResult
182	type SyncCounts = internalbridge.SyncCounts
183	type BatchSummary = internalbridge.BatchSummary
184	type ExecutionSummary = internalbridge.ExecutionSummary
185	type SyncResult = internalbridge.SyncResult
186	type PlanResult = internalbridge.PlanResult
187

1	package gitsync
2	
3	import "entire.io/entire/git-sync/internal/gitproto"
4	
5	// ErrTargetRefMoved is returned (wrapped) by Sync and Replicate when a push was
6	// rejected because the target ref changed concurrently between this run's plan
7	// and its push — a benign, retryable compare-and-swap / lease miss, not a real
8	// failure. Test for it with errors.Is(err, gitsync.ErrTargetRefMoved).
9	//
10	// On the default push path the concrete error in the chain is a
11	// *RefRejectedError (reachable with errors.As). One case does NOT carry that
12	// concrete type: a BestEffort run with ForceWithLease escalates a lease miss
13	// through a plain wrapped error that still satisfies errors.Is(ErrTargetRefMoved)
14	// but is not a *RefRejectedError. So prefer errors.Is when you only need the
15	// cause, and treat a successful errors.As(*RefRejectedError) as best-effort.
16	//
17	// This is the supported way to distinguish a racing concurrent push from a
18	// genuine push failure; prefer it over inspecting the error message text, which
19	// is free-form and server-specific.
20	var ErrTargetRefMoved = gitproto.ErrTargetRefMoved
21	
22	// RefRejectedError is a single per-ref "ng" status returned by the target's
23	// receive-pack report-status, reachable with errors.As. Ref is the rejected ref
24	// and Reason is the raw server reason text. Rejections that are concurrent
25	// target-ref moves also satisfy errors.Is(err, ErrTargetRefMoved).
26	type RefRejectedError = gitproto.RefRejectedError
27

36	)
37	
38	const (
39		protocolModeAuto = validation.ProtocolAuto
40		protocolModeV1   = validation.ProtocolV1
41		protocolModeV2   = validation.ProtocolV2
42		modeSync         = "sync"
43		modeReplicate    = "replicate"
44	)
45	
46	const DefaultMaterializedMaxObjects = materialized.DefaultMaxMaterializedObjects
47	
48	// Endpoint holds the connection configuration for a remote.
49	type Endpoint struct {
50		URL           string
51		Username      string
52		Token         string
53		BearerToken   string
54		SkipTLSVerify bool
55	
56		// FollowInfoRefsRedirect, when true, rewrites the effective host of
57		// this endpoint to the final URL of /info/refs after HTTP redirects,
58		// so follow-up upload-pack / receive-pack POSTs land on the
59		// redirected node. Plumbed into gitproto.Conn.FollowInfoRefsRedirect.
60		FollowInfoRefsRedirect bool
61	}
62	
63	// RefMapping is a user-specified source:target ref mapping.
64	type RefMapping = validation.RefMapping
65	
66	// Config holds all configuration for a sync operation.
67	type Config struct {
68		Source                 Endpoint
69		Target                 Endpoint
70		HTTPClient             *http.Client
71		Branches               []string
72		Mappings               []RefMapping
73		AllRefs                bool
74		ExcludeRefPrefixes     []string
75		IncludeTags            bool
76		DryRun                 bool
77		Verbose                bool
78		ShowStats              bool
79		MeasureMemory          bool
80		Progress               bool
81		Mode                   string
82		ForceWithLease         bool
83		ForceBlind             bool
84		Prune                  bool
85		BestEffort             bool
86		MaxPackBytes           int64
87		TargetMaxPackBytes     int64
88		TargetMaxRefUpdates    int
89		MaterializedMaxObjects int
90		ProtocolMode           string
91		BootstrapStrategy      string // "" | "first-parent" | "topo"
92	
93		// progressOut overrides the writer used by the live progress ticker.
94		// Defaults to os.Stderr when nil. Exposed for tests.
95		progressOut io.Writer
96	}
97	
98	// Re-export types from planner for CLI compatibility.
99	type (
100		RefKind    = planner.RefKind
101		Action     = planner.Action
102		BranchPlan = planner.BranchPlan
103	)
104	
105	const (
106		RefKindBranch = planner.RefKindBranch
107		RefKindTag    = planner.RefKindTag
108		RefKindOther  = planner.RefKindOther
109		ActionCreate  = planner.ActionCreate
110		ActionUpdate  = planner.ActionUpdate
111		ActionDelete  = planner.ActionDelete
112		ActionSkip    = planner.ActionSkip
113		ActionBlock   = planner.ActionBlock
114		ActionWarn    = planner.ActionWarn
115	)
116	
117	type RefInfo struct {
118		Name string        `json:"name"`
119		Hash plumbing.Hash `json:"hash"`
120	}
121	
122	func (r RefInfo) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
123		type ri struct {
124			Name string `json:"name"`
125			Hash string `json:"hash"`
126		}
127		b, err := json.Marshal(ri{Name: r.Name, Hash: r.Hash.String()})
128		if err != nil {
129			return nil, fmt.Errorf("marshal JSON: %w", err)
130		}
131		return b, nil
132	}
133	
134	// Result holds the outcome of a sync or bootstrap operation.
135	type Result struct {
136		Plans              []BranchPlan           `json:"plans"`
137		Pushed             int                    `json:"pushed"`
138		Skipped            int                    `json:"skipped"`
139		Blocked            int                    `json:"blocked"`
140		Deleted            int                    `json:"deleted"`
141		Warned             int                    `json:"warned"`
142		DryRun             bool                   `json:"dryRun"`
143		OperationMode      string                 `json:"operationMode"`
144		Relay              bool                   `json:"relay"`
145		RelayMode          string                 `json:"relayMode"`
146		RelayReason        string                 `json:"relayReason"`
147		Batching           bool                   `json:"batching"`
148		BatchCount         int                    `json:"batchCount"`
149		PlannedBatchCount  int                    `json:"plannedBatchCount"`
150		TempRefs           []string               `json:"tempRefs"`
151		BootstrapSuggested bool                   `json:"bootstrapSuggested"`
152		SourceHEAD         plumbing.ReferenceName `json:"sourceHead,omitempty"`
153		Stats              Stats                  `json:"stats"`
154		Measurement        Measurement            `json:"measurement"`
155		Protocol           string                 `json:"protocol"`
156	}
157	
158	func (r Result) Lines() []string {
159		lines := make([]string, 0, len(r.Plans)+8)
160		for _, plan := range r.Plans {
161			lines = append(lines, planner.FormatPlanLine(plan))
162		}
163		summary := fmt.Sprintf(
164			"summary: pushed=%d deleted=%d skipped=%d blocked=%d warned=%d mode=%s protocol=%s relay=%t relay-mode=%s relay-reason=%s batching=%t batch-count=%d planned-batches=%d",
165			r.Pushed, r.Deleted, r.Skipped, r.Blocked, r.Warned, r.OperationMode, r.Protocol, r.Relay, r.RelayMode, r.RelayReason, r.Batching, r.BatchCount, r.PlannedBatchCount,
166		)
167		if r.DryRun {
168			summary += " dry-run=true"
169		}
170		lines = append(lines, summary)
171		lines = append(lines, statsLines(r.Stats)...)
172		lines = append(lines, measurementLine(r.Measurement)...)
173		if r.BootstrapSuggested {
174			lines = append(lines, "hint: target refs are absent; bootstrap can seed them without local object storage")
175		}
176		if r.Batching && len(r.TempRefs) > 0 {
177			lines = append(lines, "batching: temp-refs="+strings.Join(r.TempRefs, ","))
178		}
179		if r.SourceHEAD != "" {
180			lines = append(lines, "source-head: "+r.SourceHEAD.String())
181		}
182		return lines
183	}
184	
185	// ProbeResult holds the outcome of a probe operation.
186	type ProbeResult struct {
187		SourceURL     string                 `json:"sourceUrl"`
188		TargetURL     string                 `json:"targetUrl,omitempty"`
189		RequestedMode string                 `json:"requestedMode"`
190		Protocol      string                 `json:"protocol"`
191		RefPrefixes   []string               `json:"refPrefixes"`
192		Capabilities  []string               `json:"sourceCapabilities"`
193		TargetCaps    []string               `json:"targetCapabilities,omitempty"`
194		Refs          []RefInfo              `json:"refs"`
195		SourceHEAD    plumbing.ReferenceName `json:"sourceHead,omitempty"`
196		Stats         Stats                  `json:"stats"`
197		Measurement   Measurement            `json:"measurement"`
198	}
199	
200	func (r ProbeResult) Lines() []string {
201		lines := []string{
202			"source: " + r.SourceURL,
203			"requested-protocol: " + r.RequestedMode,
204			"negotiated-protocol: " + r.Protocol,
205		}
206		if len(r.RefPrefixes) > 0 {
207			lines = append(lines, "ref-prefixes: "+strings.Join(r.RefPrefixes, ", "))
208		}
209		if r.SourceHEAD != "" {
210			lines = append(lines, "source-head: "+r.SourceHEAD.String())
211		}
212		if len(r.Capabilities) > 0 {
213			lines = append(lines, "source-capabilities: "+strings.Join(r.Capabilities, ", "))
214		}
215		if r.TargetURL != "" {
216			lines = append(lines, "target: "+r.TargetURL)
217		}
218		if len(r.TargetCaps) > 0 {
219			lines = append(lines, "target-capabilities: "+strings.Join(r.TargetCaps, ", "))
220		}
221		lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("refs: %d", len(r.Refs)))
222		for _, ref := range r.Refs {
223			lines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf("ref: %s %s", ref.Hash.String(), ref.Name))
224		}
225		lines = append(lines, statsLines(r.Stats)...)
226		lines = append(lines, measurementLine(r.Measurement)...)
227		return lines
228	}
229	
230	// FetchResult holds the outcome of a fetch operation.
231	type FetchResult struct {
232		SourceURL      string          `json:"sourceUrl"`
233		RequestedMode  string          `json:"requestedMode"`
234		Protocol       string          `json:"protocol"`
235		Wants          []RefInfo       `json:"wants"`

type RefKind = internalbridge.RefKind

const (
	RefKindBranch RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindBranch
	RefKindTag    RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindTag
	RefKindOther  RefKind = internalbridge.RefKindOther
)

type Action = internalbridge.Action

const (
	ActionCreate Action = internalbridge.ActionCreate
	ActionUpdate Action = internalbridge.ActionUpdate
	ActionDelete Action = internalbridge.ActionDelete
	ActionSkip   Action = internalbridge.ActionSkip
	ActionBlock  Action = internalbridge.ActionBlock
	ActionWarn   Action = internalbridge.ActionWarn
)

type RefResult = internalbridge.RefResult
type RefPlan = internalbridge.RefPlan
type RefInfo = internalbridge.RefInfo
type ServiceStats = internalbridge.ServiceStats
type Stats = internalbridge.Stats
type Measurement = internalbridge.Measurement
type ProbeResult = internalbridge.ProbeResult
type SyncCounts = internalbridge.SyncCounts
type BatchSummary = internalbridge.BatchSummary
type ExecutionSummary = internalbridge.ExecutionSummary
type SyncResult = internalbridge.SyncResult
type PlanResult = internalbridge.PlanResult

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "Fold internalbridge into the root package

internal/internalbridge was a pure pass-through layer: it mirrored the
public request types field-for-field, wrapped syncer.Config in an opaque
struct, and forwarded Probe/Run calls verbatim. The root client had to
convert every request through bridge mirror types, and unstable/client.go
round-tripped through them just to build a syncer.Endpoint literal.

Define the public result types (RefResult, Stats, ProbeResult, SyncResult,
...) directly in the root package instead of aliasing internal types --
this also makes their godoc visible on pkg.go.dev and exports SideBytes,
which was previously reachable through Stats.Sides but not nameable by
callers. Build syncer.Config directly in the client, matching what
unstable/client.go already does. Field sets and JSON tags are unchanged,
so the published API surface is identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git show --stat HEAD | tail -12
input.description: Commit internalbridge removal
